idea.hook: Think galaxies are the largest 'things' out there?
facts: Our Milky Way galaxy is approximately 100,000 light-years across.
Our local Laniakea Supercluster spans about 520 million light-years.
The *entire Observable Universe* stretches an incredible 93 billion light-years.
Yet, within it, lies the Hercules-Corona Borealis Great Wall...
A colossal filament of galaxies and gamma-ray bursts, estimated to be over 10 billion light-years long!
idea.payoff: That's a single cosmic structure nearly 1/10th the size of the *entire* Observable Universe. Our cosmos is structured on truly unimaginable scales.
idea.title: The UNIVERSE'S BIGGEST 'THING'?! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Think galaxies are the largest 'things' out there?
scenes.visual: Distant, swirling spiral galaxy slowly rotating in the void.

scenes.text: Milky Way: 100,000 light-years. Laniakea Supercluster: 520 million light-years.
scenes.visual: Starts with a single galaxy, then smoothly zooms out to reveal a cluster of galaxies, then a larger, web-like supercluster.

scenes.text: Observable Universe: 93 billion light-years. Yet, within it lies the...
scenes.visual: A vast, dark cosmic canvas with countless points of light (galaxies). A subtle, almost imperceptible red glow appears on one side.

scenes.text: Hercules-Corona Borealis Great Wall: Over 10 BILLION light-years long!
scenes.visual: A prominent, extremely long and dense filament of interconnected galaxies, stretching across the screen, with occasional subtle flashes representing gamma-ray bursts.

scenes.text: Nearly 1/10th the size of the *entire* Observable Universe. Truly unimaginable scales!
scenes.visual: The Great Wall visual slowly zooms out, revealing its immense scale as a single structure within the vast, awe-inspiring cosmic web, eventually fading to a deep, star-filled space.
